gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.subtitle: 5 steps to create a sample game

step1.title: Define Scenes
step1.description: Scenes are the top-level unit for managing each screen or stage of your game.
details: Enter a scene name in the Scene List panel and click "+" to create a new scene.
Switch between scenes by clicking their names.
You can rename a scene by double-clicking it.
tips: Use clear names like "TitleScreen", "Stage1", "GameOver" to organize your game.

step2.title: Define Actors
step2.description: Actors are the objects in your game – characters, enemies, backgrounds, UI elements, etc.
details: Right-click on the Node Canvas to add a new actor.
Drag between actor nodes to create data-flow connections.
Click an actor to open the Component Picker.
tips: Use Ctrl+D to quickly duplicate actors.
Save frequently-used actors as Prefabs for reuse.

step3.title: Select & Define Modules
step3.description: Modules (Components) define an actor's capabilities – physics, rendering, input handling, etc.
details: Click an actor to open the Component Picker and attach modules.
Use the Component Editor to define new custom modules.
Each module has Variables (state), Tasks (behavior), and Dependencies.
tips: Filter by category (UI, Logic, System, GameObject) to find modules faster.
Check dependencies to ensure required modules are attached.

step4.title: Implement Code
step4.description: Generate code from your actor/component definitions using AI assistance.
details: Use the code generation feature to produce implementation code.
The MCP Server can assist with code review and refinement.
Preview generated code before applying it.
tips: Use the MCP Server for iterative code improvements.

step5.title: Run & Test
step5.description: Test your game by running and iterating on the generated output.
details: Build and run your project to test functionality.
Use the Preview panel to verify scene structure.
Iterate on components and re-generate code as needed.
tips: Use the Preview panel to verify your actor hierarchy.

settings.title: Settings
settings.languageDescription: Select the display language for the editor.

sceneList.description: Scenes are the top-level unit for managing each screen or stage of your game.
sceneList.item1: Enter a name and click "+" to create a new scene
sceneList.item2: Click a scene name to select and switch editing target

nodeCanvas.description: The main editor area for visually placing and connecting actors (game objects).
nodeCanvas.item1: Right-click to add actors from the context menu
nodeCanvas.item2: Drag between nodes to connect data flows
nodeCanvas.item3: Mouse wheel to zoom, drag to pan
nodeCanvas.item7: Click an actor to open the Component Picker

componentEditor.description: A panel for defining and editing component (module) details.
componentEditor.item2: Select a category: UI / Logic / System / GameObject
componentEditor.item4: "+ Variable" to add state variables (name, type, default)
componentEditor.item5: "+ Task" to add tasks (define input/output ports)
componentEditor.item7: Save to apply, Cancel to discard changes

componentList.description: A panel for listing and managing all components in the project.
componentList.item1: Type in the search bar to filter by name or domain
componentList.item2: Use category buttons to filter by category
componentList.item3: Click an item to expand and view variables, tasks, and dependencies
componentList.item4: "Edit" button to open the Component Editor
componentList.item7: "+ New Component" to create a new component

prefabList.description: A panel for managing reusable actor templates (prefabs).
prefabList.item1: Right-click an actor on Canvas → "Save as Prefab" to register
prefabList.item3: "+" button to place an instance in the active scene

preview.description: A preview panel to inspect the actor hierarchy and connections in the current scene.

toolbar.title: Toolbar
toolbar.description: The menu bar for project operations and panel visibility toggles.
toolbar.item1: New – Create a new project (current project is discarded)
toolbar.item6: Panel toggles – Show/hide Components / Prefabs / Behavior / Preview
toolbar.item7: Push – Push changes to a GitHub repository

componentPicker.description: A dialog for selecting components to attach to an actor.
componentPicker.item2: Type in the search bar to filter by name or domain
componentPicker.item3: Use category buttons to filter by category
componentPicker.item4: ⚠ Dependency warnings appear when required components are missing

sequenceEditor.title: Sequence Editor
sequenceEditor.description: An editor for defining ordered steps in a sequence actor.
sequenceEditor.item1: Enter a step name and click "+ Add Step"
sequenceEditor.item3: Click a step name to edit it inline
sequenceEditor.item4: Add a description for each step

behaviorEditor.description: An editor for defining scene states and key bindings.
behaviorEditor.item1: Enter a name and click "+" to create a new state
behaviorEditor.item2: Click a state tab to switch the active state
behaviorEditor.item3: Capture button – directly input a keyboard key
behaviorEditor.item4: List button – select from a list of common keys
behaviorEditor.item5: Description – describe the action in natural language
behaviorEditor.item6: Target Actor – specify the target actor for the key action
behaviorEditor.item7: Delete State – delete the current state

subScenePicker.title: SubScene Picker
subScenePicker.description: A dialog for assigning a sub-scene to a scene actor.
subScenePicker.item1: Click a scene name to set it as the sub-scene
subScenePicker.item2: Select (None) to clear the sub-scene assignment

setup.title: Initial Setup
setup.subtitle: Configure a secrets provider to get started.
setup.providerDescription: Choose a secrets management provider for storing API keys, OAuth credentials, and other secrets.
setup.awsSsmDescription: AWS-native parameter store. Credentials via environment variables or IAM role.
setup.infisicalDescription: Self-hosted or cloud secrets manager with Machine Identity authentication.
setup.awsEnvVarsDescription: Set these environment variables before starting the server:
setup.awsEnvVarsAlt: Or use IAM role / ~/.aws/credentials for credential-based auth.
setup.awsRegionHelp: Optional — falls back to AWS_REGION / AWS_DEFAULT_REGION env var.
setup.awsPathPrefixHelp: SSM parameter name prefix. Secrets are stored as {prefix}/shared/{KEY}.
setup.infisicalCredentialsHelp: Create a Machine Identity with Universal Auth in Infisical Dashboard > Project Settings > Machine Identities.
setup.saveSuccess: Configuration saved to {{path}}. The server will restart automatically.

archetypeWizard.title: Game Archetype Wizard
archetypeWizard.selectArchetype: Select a game archetype for your project.
archetypeWizard.selectDesign: Choose design patterns to add modules.
archetypeWizard.noDesigns: No design patterns available for this archetype.

platformer.name: 2D Platformer
platformer.desc: Side-scrolling action game with jumping, running, and collecting.

rpg.name: RPG
rpg.desc: Role-playing game with character progression, combat, and quests.

visualNovel.name: Visual Novel
visualNovel.desc: Story-driven game with branching choices, character portraits, and backgrounds.

puzzle.name: Puzzle Game
puzzle.desc: Logic and puzzle-based game with grids, scoring, and timers.

cardGame.name: Card Game
cardGame.desc: Card-based game with deck building, hand management, and turns.

action.name: Action Game
action.desc: Real-time action with weapons, enemy AI, and wave-based spawning.

simulation.name: Simulation
simulation.desc: Management simulation with resource management, building, and time progression.

physicsMovement.name: Physics-Based Movement
physicsMovement.desc: Character control with gravity, friction, and jumping.

turnBasedCombat.name: Turn-Based Combat
turnBasedCombat.desc: Turn-based battle system with command selection and stat management.

branchingNarrative.name: Branching Narrative
branchingNarrative.desc: Script engine and story branching through player choices.

characterPortraits.name: Character Portraits
characterPortraits.desc: Character display, expression changes, and text box management.

saveLoad.name: Save & Load
saveLoad.desc: Game state save and restore system.

gridBased.name: Grid-Based
gridBased.desc: Grid placement and match detection puzzle mechanics.

deckBuilding.name: Deck Building
deckBuilding.desc: Deck shuffling, drawing, and hand management.

weaponSystem.name: Weapon System
weaponSystem.desc: Weapon firing, reloading, and hit detection.

enemyAi.name: Enemy AI
enemyAi.desc: State-based enemy AI patterns: detection, pursuit, and attack.

resourceManagement.name: Resource Management
resourceManagement.desc: Collection and consumption of gold, wood, stone, and other resources.

archetype.title: Selecting an Archetype
archetype.desc: Choose a game genre. Core modules will be automatically prepared based on your selection. You can add features later through design patterns.

design.title: Selecting Design Patterns
design.desc: Choose design patterns (plugins) suited to your archetype. Recommended patterns are pre-selected but can be changed. Each pattern injects corresponding modules into your project.

modules.title: Module Injection
modules.desc: Review the modules (components) to be injected based on your selections. Each module has variables (v) and tasks (t). Click "Inject" to add them as components to your project.

ai.archetypeQuestion: I'm new to game development. Which archetype should I choose? Please explain the characteristics of each.
ai.designQuestion: When building a {{archetype}}, which design patterns work best together?
ai.modulesQuestion: Please briefly explain the role and usage of each of the {{count}} modules about to be injected.
ai.placeholder: This feature will be available when connected to an AI backend. Currently in preview mode. Please also use the Help button and Google Search button in the archetype wizard.

domainDiagram.emptyPrompt: Select a scene to view the domain diagram

description.domain: Shows how domains (actors) message each other — who sends what to whom. Scene transitions are shown as dashed links.
description.system: Shows how system/logic modules attach to domains — which functions affect which domain, and what they do.
description.ui: Shows which UI components display which domain information — what data the UI reads from each domain.
description.detail: Detail view — all objects attached to this actor: components, connections, tasks, variables, and scene transitions.

codegen.title: Code Generation Bridge
codegen.platformDesc: Select the target platform for code generation.
codegen.formatDesc: Choose what to generate alongside the source code.
codegen.dryRun: Dry run (generate prompts only, do not launch Claude Code)
codegen.scopeDesc: Select specific scenes and components to generate. Leave empty to generate all.
codegen.scopeHint: If nothing is selected, all scenes and components will be included.
codegen.noTasks: No tasks to generate. Add scenes or components to the project first.
